title: oxalis bulbigera
intro: Oxalis bulbigera, also known as the small-flowered wood-sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood-sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). Traditionally, Oxalis bulbigera has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. Oxalis bulbigera is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it can also be consumed fresh in salads or used as a garnish, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
